name: Senya de Lloser y Seguili — circular desde Orba
region_intro: Hiking around Orba offers diverse landscapes in the province of Alicante, characterized by impressive mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Segaria and Sierra de Bernia. The region features green valleys, extensive orange and lemon groves, and olive and almond orchards. Water features like the Riu Girona and various natural springs are also present along many routes. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences from gentle valley walks to more challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Orba The most popular hiking route is Senya de Lloser y Seguili — circular desde Orba, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate path crosses the mountain, offering views of surrounding towns and peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Castellet d'Orba Trail (SL-CV 92), a moderate 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This route leads to the ancient castle ruins of El Castellet d'Orba, providing panoramic views of the valley and distant Mediterranean Sea. Local hikers also love the Cherry Orchard – Capilla de Campell loop from Orba, an 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through varied scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Orba is defined by mountain ranges, lush valleys with citrus groves, and river features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Orba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Orba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Orba?
A: Orba offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from gentle valley walks through citrus groves to more challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra de Segaria and Sierra de Bernia. You'll find routes along the Riu Girona, past natural springs, and through lush valleys with olive and almond orchards.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Orba?
A: Yes, Orba has several easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23526301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Fonts de Dalt – San Luis Beltrán Church loop from Orba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.1 km trail with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed stroll.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Orba area?
A: Many of Orba's trails are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357874985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senya de Lloser y Seguili — circular desde Orba&lt;/a&gt;, which crosses the mountain and provides views of surrounding towns and peaks.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to historical sites or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357832259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castellet d'Orba Trail (SL-CV 92)&lt;/a&gt; leads to the ancient castle ruins of El Castellet d'Orba, offering spectacular panoramic views of the valley and the distant Mediterranean Sea. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/778482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet d'Orba Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt; directly.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Orba?
A: Orba enjoys a typical Mediterranean climate with abundant sunshine, making it an enjoyable destination year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing pleasant conditions.

Q: Are there any trails with water features or waterfalls nearby?
A: Yes, the region has several natural water sources. You might discover springs like Font de la Xopera or Font dels Caus along various routes. Additionally, the well-known &lt;strong&gt;Les Fonts d'Algar&lt;/strong&gt;, a series of natural waterfalls and pools, is located just 13.5 km from Orba and makes for a great excursion.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Orba?
A: Hikers in Orba can expect a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll traverse green valleys, often referred to as the 'Orange Valley' due to extensive orange and lemon groves, alongside olive and almond orchards. The routes also offer views of impressive mountain ranges like the Sierra de Segaria and Sierra de Bernia.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Orba?
A: The routes in Orba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse scenery, from lush groves to panoramic mountain views, and the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Orba?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the easier and moderate circular routes through the valleys and groves are suitable for families. The shorter, less strenuous paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23526301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Fonts de Dalt – San Luis Beltrán Church loop&lt;/a&gt;, are good options for families with children.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trailheads in Orba?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the village centers where many trails begin. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps that often indicate parking areas close to popular starting points for routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1027186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cherry Orchard – Capilla de Campell loop from Orba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any other natural attractions or parks to explore near Orba?
A: Beyond Orba's immediate trails, you can visit the nearby &lt;strong&gt;Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt; for hiking and birdwatching, or the &lt;strong&gt;Montgo Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which also offers excellent hiking opportunities. For a unique experience, the &lt;strong&gt;Cueva de las Calaveras (Prehistoric Cave)&lt;/strong&gt; is just 4.2 km away.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hiking trails in Orba?
A: Orba offers a range of difficulties. Out of approximately 27 trails, there are 8 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 6 more difficult options. This variety ensures there's something for every fitness level, from leisurely strolls to more challenging ascents.
tips: Coinciding with Senya de Lloser there is an interesting path that crosses the mountain with the towns in the background, on one side, and the peaks, with the Coveta de Seguili, on the other.
Old rural stone shelters used by shepherds to seek shelter from the heat and water for their animals.
